title: Two hours, zero fish
summary: An eight-year-old boy and his uncle have been fishing for two hours and caught nothing. The uncle says they have caught something else.

body.speaker: Uncle
body.text: Be quiet, Kien. Fish hear everything.

body.speaker: Kien
body.text: Uncle, fish do not have ears.

body.speaker: Uncle
body.text: They have a small thing like an ear. They feel the water move. Be quiet.

body.speaker: Kien
body.text: How long have we sat here?

body.speaker: Uncle
body.text: Two hours.

body.speaker: Kien
body.text: And we have caught zero fish.

body.speaker: Uncle
body.text: We have caught one thing. We do not see it yet.

body.speaker: Kien
body.text: What is the thing?

body.speaker: Uncle
body.text: Patience.

body.speaker: Kien
body.text: I am hungry.

body.speaker: Uncle
body.text: Eat the rice. It is in the bag.

body.speaker: Kien
body.text: I want fish, not rice.

body.speaker: Uncle
body.text: Then we will sit longer.

body.speaker: Kien
body.text: Uncle, you are not angry?

body.speaker: Uncle
body.text: No. When I was small, I asked my father the same question. He told me to be quiet too.

body.speaker: Kien
body.text: Did you catch a fish?

body.speaker: Uncle
body.text: Yes. After four hours. I was eight. I still remember the fish.

body.speaker: Kien
body.text: I am eight too.

body.speaker: Uncle
body.text: Then you have two hours left.

body.speaker: Kien
body.text: And if no fish comes?

body.speaker: Uncle
body.text: Then we go home with the same fish I went home with the day before I caught one.

body.speaker: Kien
body.text: Which is zero?

body.speaker: Uncle
body.text: Which is the lesson. Bring it home. It is still in your bag.

body.speaker: Kien
body.text: Okay. I am quiet now.

body.speaker: Uncle
body.text: Good. Look at the water. It is telling us something.

grammarNotes.title: Present perfect: 'have/has + past participle'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng thì hiện tại hoàn thành (have/has + V3) để nói về việc đã xảy ra tính đến hiện tại. Trong bài: 'How long have we sat here?' / 'We have caught zero fish.' Công thức: chủ ngữ + have/has + động từ dạng quá khứ phân từ (sit → sat, catch → caught).
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the present perfect (have/has + past participle) for actions that happened up to now. From the dialogue: 'How long have we sat here?' / 'We have caught zero fish.' Form: subject + have/has + past participle (sit → sat, catch → caught).

cloze.template:
Uncle: Be quiet, Kien. Fish hear everything.
Kien: Uncle, fish do not have {{b1}}.
Uncle: They have a small thing like an ear. They feel the {{b2}} move. Be quiet.
Kien: How long have we sat here?
Uncle: {{b3}} hours.
Kien: And we have caught {{b4}} fish.
Uncle: We have caught one thing. We do not see it yet.
Kien: What is the thing?
Uncle: {{b5}}.
Kien: I am {{b6}}.
Uncle: Eat the {{b7}}. It is in the bag.
Kien: I want fish, not rice.
Uncle: Then we will sit longer.
Kien: Uncle, you are not angry?
Uncle: No. When I was small, I asked my {{b8}} the same question. He told me to be quiet too.
Kien: Did you catch a fish?
Uncle: Yes. After {{b9}} hours. I was {{b10}}. I still remember the fish.
Kien: I am eight too.
Uncle: Then you have two hours left.
Kien: And if no fish comes?
Uncle: Then we go home with the same fish I went home with the day before I caught one.
Kien: Which is zero?
Uncle: Which is the lesson. Bring it home. It is still in your bag.
Kien: Okay. I am {{b11}} now.
Uncle: Good. Look at the water. It is {{b12}} us something.

blanks.explanation: Kien protests that fish do not have ears — though the uncle replies they have something like one.
Fish 'feel the water move' — that is how they sense sound.
'Two hours' — how long they have been sitting.
The uncle's answer: they have caught patience, even if no fish.
Kien is hungry — a normal A1 child's complaint.
Eat the rice — what is in the bag.
When the uncle was small, he asked his father the same question.
'After four hours' — that was how long the uncle had to wait.
'I was eight' — the same age as Kien now.
Kien decides to be quiet — finally agreeing with his uncle's first instruction.
'The water is telling us something' — the uncle's final image.

translationVi:
Chú: Yên lặng, Kiên. Cá nghe được hết đấy.
Kiên: Chú ơi, cá làm gì có tai.
Chú: Cá có một bộ phận giống tai. Chúng cảm nhận nước chuyển động. Yên đi.
Kiên: Mình ngồi đây bao lâu rồi chú?
Chú: Hai tiếng.
Kiên: Và mình bắt được không con cá nào.
Chú: Mình bắt được một thứ. Chỉ là chưa thấy thôi.
Kiên: Thứ gì hả chú?
Chú: Sự kiên nhẫn.
Kiên: Cháu đói.
Chú: Ăn cơm đi. Cơm trong túi đấy.
Kiên: Cháu muốn cá, không phải cơm.
Chú: Thế mình ngồi lâu hơn nữa.
Kiên: Chú không bực hả chú?
Chú: Không. Hồi nhỏ, chú cũng hỏi bố chú đúng câu đó. Bố chú cũng bảo chú yên lặng.
Kiên: Rồi chú có bắt được cá không?
Chú: Có. Sau bốn tiếng. Chú lúc đó tám tuổi. Đến giờ vẫn còn nhớ con cá đó.
Kiên: Cháu cũng tám tuổi.
Chú: Vậy cháu còn hai tiếng nữa.
Kiên: Còn nếu không có cá thì sao?
Chú: Thì mình về nhà với con cá y như cái ngày trước khi chú bắt được con cá đầu tiên.
Kiên: Tức là không có cá?
Chú: Tức là bài học. Đem nó về. Nó vẫn ở trong túi cháu.
Kiên: Được rồi. Cháu im rồi đây.
Chú: Tốt. Nhìn xuống nước đi. Nó đang nói với mình một điều gì đó.

questions.prompt: Where are Kien and his uncle?
questions.explanation: The whole conversation is about catching fish and listening to the water.
questions.hint: Look at what they keep talking about.

questions.prompt: How long have they been sitting there?
questions.hint: Look at the uncle's short reply.

questions.prompt: How many fish have they caught?
What does the uncle say they have caught instead?
questions.hint: Look at the uncle's short answer.

questions.prompt: What does Kien want when he is hungry?
options: Fish — not the rice in the bag
questions.hint: Look at Kien's complaint after his uncle offers rice.

questions.prompt: How long did the uncle have to wait, when he was a boy, for his first fish?
questions.hint: Look at the uncle's story about his own childhood.

questions.prompt: How old was the uncle when he caught his first fish?
questions.hint: Same turn as the previous question.

questions.prompt: Why does the uncle say Kien has 'two hours left'?
options: Then they have to leave the river
Because Kien is eight and the uncle waited four hours at that age — two more hours to match
Because dinner is in two hours
questions.explanation: The uncle waited four hours; they have already sat for two; so Kien has two hours to match.
questions.hint: Look at the numbers in the uncle's story.

questions.prompt: What does the uncle mean by 'It is still in your bag'?
options: The fish is in the bag
The lesson (patience, even if no fish) is still with you
There is more rice in the bag
questions.explanation: Even without a fish, they take home a lesson — and that is the point.
questions.hint: Look at what 'the lesson' refers to.

questions.prompt: What is the tone of the conversation?
options: Warm and patient — the uncle teaches Kien without scolding, and Kien finally agrees to be quiet
questions.explanation: The uncle is not angry; he tells his own childhood story. Kien ends by saying 'I am quiet now.'
questions.hint: Notice how the uncle answers each complaint.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The uncle says they have caught 'patience', even though they caught no fish. Think of a time you did something but felt you 'got nothing'. What other thing might you have got that you didn't notice at the time?
